erne: noun. A sea eagle (Haliaeetus), especially the white-tailed eagle (Haliaeetus albicilla)
eve: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Even.
evener: adjective. comparative form of even: more even
ewe: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Ewe.
ewer: noun. A kind of widemouthed pitcher or jug with a shape like a vase and a handle, and originally used for carrying water.
hen: noun. A female chicken (Gallus gallus), especially a sexually mature one kept for her eggs.
her: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Herero.
here: adverb. In, on, or at this place (a place perceived to be close to the speaker); compare there.
hew: verb. To chop away at; to whittle down; to mow down.
hewn: adjective. Made or crafted by cutting, whittling down.
nee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kumak.
nerve: noun. A bundle of neurons with their connective tissue sheaths, blood vessels and lymphatics.
never: adverb. At no time; on no occasion; in no circumstance.
new: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Newar.
newer: adjective. comparative form of new: more new, more recent.
ree: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rejang Kayan.
reeve: noun. Any of several local officials, with varying responsibilities.
renew: verb. To make (something) new again; to restore to freshness or original condition.
rev: verb. To increase the speed of a motor, or to operate at a higher speed.
vee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter V / v.
veer: verb. To let out (a sail-line), to allow (a sheet) to run out.
veneer: noun. A thin decorative covering of fine material (usually wood) applied to coarser wood or other material.
weever: noun. Any of the usually brown fish in family Trachinidae, which catch prey by burying themselves in the sand and snatching them as they go
wen: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Sorbian languages.
were: verb. second-person singular simple past indicative of be
whee: interjection. An expression of pleasure or enjoyment, mostly from rapid physical motion.
wheen: noun. A little; a small number.
where: adverb. In, at or to what place.
wherve: noun. Alternative form of wharve (“whorl of a spindle”).
wren: noun. Troglodytes troglodytes (Eurasian wren, type species and sole Old World species of the family Troglodytidae).
enew: verb. To drive back to the water; to pursue.
evene: verb. To occur; to happen; to come to pass.
heer: noun. A yarn measure of six hundred yards, or 1/24 of a spindle.
hewe: noun. A domestic; a servant or retainer.
reen: noun. A running waterway that links a ditch or stream to a river.
rew: noun. Obsolete or dialectal form of row
wer: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Weri.
weren: verb. plural simple past of be
